title: Ameresco and Republic Services Bring 5.2 MWe Upper Rock Landfill Gas-to-Renewable Natural Gas Facility Online
content: Ameresco, Inc. and Republic Services, Inc. have announced the operational launch of a 5.2 MWe Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) facility at the Upper Rock Island County Landfill in East Moline, Illinois. The facility, which began operations in December, captures raw landfill gas and processes 2,000 standard cubic feet per minute (scfm) into pipeline-quality RNG. This project is expected to produce over 500,000 Dekatherms annually and reduce COâ‚‚ emissions by more than 27,000 metric tons per year. As the 16th operational collaboration between the two companies, the facility supports Illinois' target of 40% renewable energy by 2030 and Republic Servicesâ€™ goal to increase biogas reuse by 50%. Michael Bakas, Amerescoâ€™s President of Renewable Fuels, highlighted the project as a scalable model for the circular economy, displacing fossil fuels and benefiting local communities.
author: Analysts Account
